content:
# Exodus Chapter 38 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 109–110; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 81–82 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_038_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_038_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_038_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_038_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — CONSTRUCTION AND ACCOUNTING:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 38 across pages 109–110, covering two distinct sections: the construction of the bronze altar, laver, and courtyard enclosure (vv. 1–20), followed by the material accounting of the tabernacle (vv. 21–31). The shift at verse 21 is marked by the phrase "these are the reckonings of the tabernacle" (elleh fequdei ha-mishkan), introducing a meticulous census of gold, silver, and bronze. This is the only passage in the construction narrative that quantifies materials and links silver to the census half-shekel. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 81–82, CAP. XXVIII continuing) broadly parallels the MT here, since the bronze altar and courtyard construction in MT 38 correspond to LXX 38:1–7 and surrounding verses. However, Vaticanus interleaves priestly-garment material (corresponding to MT 39) within its chapter 38, and the material accounting section shows some variation in ordering and numerical details. The LXX uses τάλαντον for "talent" (kikkar) throughout.

---

## The Bronze Altar (vv. 1–7)

**1** And he made [va-ya'as] the altar of [et-mizbach] the burnt-offering [ha-olah] of acacia wood [atsei shittim]; five cubits [khamesh ammot] its length [orkho] and five cubits [ve-khamesh ammot] its width [rokhbo] — square [ravua] — and three cubits [ve-shalosh ammot] its height [qomato].

> **Key terms:** מִזְבַּח הָעֹלָה (mizbach ha-olah) = "altar of the burnt-offering" — the bronze altar for the outer court, distinct from the golden incense altar (37:25). רָבוּעַ (ravua) = "square" — foursquare in its footprint. **LXX:** θυσιαστήριον τὸ χαλκοῦν ("the bronze altar"); Vaticanus parallels here in LXX 38:1.

**2** And he made [va-ya'as] its horns [qarnotav] on its four corners [al arba pinnotav] — from it [mimmenu] were its horns [hayu qarnotav] — and he overlaid it [va-yetsap oto] with bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Key terms:** קַרְנֹתָיו (qarnotav) = "its horns" — projections at each corner; the horns are integral to the altar ("from it"), not attached separately. נְחֹשֶׁת (nekhoshet) = "bronze/copper." **LXX:** κέρατα ("horns") — ἐκ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου ("from the altar"), matching the integral construction.

**3** And he made [va-ya'as] all the vessels of [et-kol-klei] the altar [ha-mizbeakh]: the pots [ha-sirot] and the shovels [ve-et-ha-ya'im] and the basins [ve-et-ha-mizraqot] and the forks [ve-et-ha-mizlagot] and the fire-pans [ve-et-ha-makhtot]; all its vessels [kol-kelav] he made [asah] of bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Key terms:** סִירֹת (sirot) = "pots" — for ash removal. יָעִים (ya'im) = "shovels." מִזְרָקֹת (mizraqot) = "basins" — for tossing/sprinkling blood. מִזְלָגֹת (mizlagot) = "forks/flesh-hooks." מַחְתֹּת (makhtot) = "fire-pans/censers." **LXX:** lists the same five implements.

**4** And he made [va-ya'as] for the altar [la-mizbeakh] a grating [mikhbar], a work of [ma'aseh] network [reshet] of bronze [nekhoshet], under [takhat] its ledge [karkubbo], from beneath [mi-lemattah], until [ad] its middle [khetsyo].

> **Key terms:** מִכְבָּר (mikhbar) = "grating" — from כבר ("to sieve/interlace"). כַּרְכֻּבּוֹ (karkubbo) = "its ledge/rim" — the projecting shelf around the altar's midsection. **LXX:** παράθεμα ("something placed beside") for the grating structure.

**5** And he cast [va-yitsoq] four rings [arba tabe'ot] on the four ends [be-arba ha-qetsavot] of the grating of [le-mikhbar] bronze [ha-nekhoshet], holders [batim] for the poles [la-baddim].

> **Key terms:** בָּתִּים (batim) = "holders/housings" — literally "houses"; receptacles for the carrying poles. **LXX:** δακτυλίους ("rings") — τοῖς φορεῖσιν ("for the carrying-bars").

**6** And he made [va-ya'as] the poles [et-ha-baddim] of acacia wood [atsei shittim], and he overlaid them [va-yetsap otam] with bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Key terms:** בַּדִּים (baddim) = "poles" — bronze-overlaid, unlike the gold-overlaid poles of the ark and table. **LXX:** matches.

**7** And he brought [va-yave] the poles [et-ha-baddim] into the rings [ba-tabe'ot] on the sides of [al tsal'ot] the altar [ha-mizbeakh], to carry [laset] it [oto] with them [bahem]; hollow [nevuv] of boards [lukhot] he made it [asah oto].

> **Key terms:** נְבוּב לֻחֹת (nevuv lukhot) = "hollow of boards/planks" — the altar is not solid but a frame of acacia planks, hollow inside. **LXX:** κοῖλον σανιδωτόν ("hollow, made of planks") — exact match.

---

## The Bronze Laver (v. 8)

**8** And he made [va-ya'as] the laver [et-ha-kiyyor] of bronze [nekhoshet] and its stand [ve-et-kanno] of bronze [nekhoshet], with the mirrors of [be-mar'ot] the women-who-served [ha-tsove'ot] who served [asher tsave'u] at the entrance of [petakh] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed].

> **Key terms:** כִּיּוֹר (kiyyor) = "laver/basin" — for priestly washing. מַרְאֹת הַצֹּבְאֹת (mar'ot ha-tsove'ot) = "mirrors of the serving-women" — polished bronze mirrors donated by women who assembled/served at the tent entrance. The root צבא here means "to serve/assemble" (cf. 1 Sam 2:22). **LXX:** ἐκ τῶν κατόπτρων τῶν νηστευσασῶν ("from the mirrors of the fasting-women") — Vaticanus reads νηστευσασῶν ("those who fasted") rather than MT's "those who served," a significant interpretive divergence.

---

## The Courtyard (vv. 9–20)

**9** And he made [va-ya'as] the courtyard [et-he-khatser]; for the south side [li-fe'at negev teimanah], the hangings of [qal'ei] the courtyard [he-khatser] of twisted linen [shesh moshzar], a hundred cubits [me'ah ba-ammah].

> **Key terms:** חָצֵר (khatser) = "courtyard/enclosure." קַלְעֵי (qal'ei) = "hangings/curtains" — from קלע ("to sling/hang"); the linen screens forming the courtyard walls. שֵׁשׁ מׇשְׁזָר (shesh moshzar) = "twisted/braided linen." **LXX:** αὐλήν ("court") — ἐκ βύσσου κεκλωσμένης ("of spun linen").

**10** Their pillars [ammudeihem] — twenty [esrim], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — twenty [esrim], of bronze [nekhoshet]; the hooks of [vavei] the pillars [ha-ammudim] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] — silver [kesef].

> **Key terms:** וָוֵי (vavei) = "hooks" — literally the letter-name vav, shaped like hooks. חֲשֻׁקֵיהֶם (khashukeihem) = "their bands/fillets" — the silver connectors binding pillars together. **LXX:** matches with ἀγκύλαι ("hooks") and ψαλίδες or similar.

**11** And for the north side [ve-li-fe'at tsafon], a hundred cubits [me'ah ba-ammah]; their pillars [ammudeihem] — twenty [esrim], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — twenty [esrim], of bronze [nekhoshet]; the hooks of [vavei] the pillars [ha-ammudim] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] — silver [kesef].

> **Key terms:** צָפוֹן (tsafon) = "north." Identical dimensions and fittings to the south side, reflecting the courtyard's symmetry. **LXX:** matches.

**12** And for the west side [ve-li-fe'at yam], hangings [qela'im] — fifty cubits [khamishim ba-ammah]; their pillars [ammudeihem] — ten [asarah], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — ten [asarah]; the hooks of [vavei] the pillars [ha-ammudim] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] — silver [kesef].

> **Key terms:** יָם (yam) = "sea" — used for "west" because the Mediterranean lies west of Israel. **LXX:** θαλάσσης ("sea") — same idiom in Greek.

**13** And for the east side [ve-li-fe'at qedmah] — eastward [mizrakhah] — fifty cubits [khamishim ammah].

> **Key terms:** קֵדְמָה מִזְרָחָה (qedmah mizrakhah) = "eastward, toward the sunrise" — doubled directional marker; the entrance faces east. **LXX:** πρὸς ἀνατολάς ("toward the rising").

**14** Hangings [qela'im] — fifteen cubits [khamesh-esreh ammah] — to the shoulder [la-katef]; their pillars [ammudeihem] — three [sheloshah], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — three [sheloshah].

> **Key terms:** כָּתֵף (katef) = "shoulder" — the flanking section beside the gate opening. **LXX:** τὸ κατὰ νώτου ("the part along the back/shoulder").

**15** And for the second shoulder [ve-la-katef ha-shenit] — from this side and from that side [mi-zeh u-mi-zeh] of the gate of the courtyard [le-sha'ar he-khatser] — hangings [qela'im] — fifteen cubits [khamesh-esreh ammah]; their pillars [ammudeihem] — three [sheloshah], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — three [sheloshah].

> **Key terms:** שַׁעַר הֶחָצֵר (sha'ar he-khatser) = "gate of the courtyard" — the single entrance on the east side, flanked by two equal curtain sections. **LXX:** matches.

**16** All the hangings of [kol-qal'ei] the courtyard [he-khatser] all around [saviv] — twisted linen [shesh moshzar].

> **Key terms:** The summary statement: the entire courtyard perimeter is of uniform twisted linen — pure white enclosure. **LXX:** matches.

**17** And the bases [ve-ha-adanim] for the pillars [la-ammudim] — bronze [nekhoshet]; the hooks of [vavei] the pillars [ha-ammudim] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] — silver [kesef]; and the overlaying of [ve-tsippui] their tops [rasheihem] — silver [kesef]; and they [ve-hem] were banded [mekhushshaqim] with silver [kesef] — all the pillars of [kol ammudei] the courtyard [he-khatser].

> **Key terms:** מְחֻשָּׁקִים (mekhushshaqim) = "banded/filleted" — Pual participle of חשׁק; every pillar wrapped with silver connectors. צִפּוּי רָאשֵׁיהֶם (tsippui rasheihem) = "the overlaying of their heads" — silver capitals atop each pillar. **LXX:** matches with κεφαλίδες ("capitals") of silver.

**18** And the screen [u-masakh] of the gate of [sha'ar] the courtyard [he-khatser] — a work of [ma'aseh] an embroiderer [roqem] — of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar]; and twenty cubits [ve-esrim ammah] — length [orekh], and height [ve-qomah] — in the width [va-rokhav] — five cubits [khamesh ammot], corresponding to [le-ummat] the hangings of [qal'ei] the courtyard [he-khatser].

> **Key terms:** רֹקֵם (roqem) = "embroiderer" — distinguished from חֹשֵׁב ("designer/weaver"); a lesser grade of textile artistry. The gate screen alone has color; the rest of the courtyard is plain white linen. **LXX:** ποικιλτοῦ ("of a variegator") — matching the embroidery distinction.

**19** And their pillars [ve-ammudeihem] — four [arba'ah], and their bases [ve-adneihem] — four [arba'ah] — bronze [nekhoshet]; their hooks [vaveihem] — silver [kesef], and the overlaying of [ve-tsippui] their tops [rasheihem] and their bands [va-khashukeihem] — silver [kesef].

> **Key terms:** Four gate pillars (vs. three per shoulder section); silver-topped with bronze bases — the consistent metallic hierarchy of the courtyard. **LXX:** matches.

**20** And all the pegs [ve-khol-ha-yetedot] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan] and for the courtyard [ve-la-khatser] all around [saviv] — bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Key terms:** יְתֵדֹת (yetedot) = "pegs/stakes" — tent pegs anchoring the guy-ropes of the entire structure. Bronze throughout — the humblest fittings are the most utilitarian metal. **LXX:** πάσσαλοι ("pegs") — matches.

---

## The Accounting of the Tabernacle (vv. 21–31)

**21** These are the reckonings of [elleh fequdei] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] — the tabernacle of [mishkan] the testimony [ha-edut] — which were reckoned [asher puqqad] by the mouth of [al-pi] Moses [Mosheh], the service of [avodat] the Levites [ha-Levi'im] by the hand of [be-yad] Ithamar [Itamar], son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the priest [ha-kohen].

> **Key terms:** פְקוּדֵי (fequdei) = "reckonings/accountings" — from פקד ("to visit/number/reckon"); this is the parashah name (Pekudei). מִשְׁכַּן הָעֵדֻת (mishkan ha-edut) = "tabernacle of the testimony" — linking the structure to the covenant tablets within. **LXX:** ἡ ἐπίσκεψις τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("the visitation/reckoning of the tent of the testimony").

**22** And Bezalel [u-Vetsal'el] son of [ben] Uri [Uri] son of [ben] Hur [Khur], of the tribe of [le-matteh] Judah [Yehudah], made [asah] all [et-kol] that [asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh].

> **Key terms:** Full genealogy: Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur — three generations linking the chief craftsman to the tribe of Judah. **LXX:** Βεσελεηλ — matches.

**23** And with him [ve-itto] — Oholiab [Oholiav] son of [ben] Ahisamach [Akhisamakh], of the tribe of [le-matteh] Dan [Dan], an engraver [kharash] and a designer [ve-khoshev] and an embroiderer [ve-roqem] in the blue [ba-tekhelet] and in the purple [u-va-argaman] and in the scarlet yarn [u-ve-tola'at ha-shani] and in the linen [u-va-shesh].

> **Key terms:** חָרָשׁ (kharash) = "engraver/craftsman." חֹשֵׁב (khoshev) = "designer/pattern-weaver" — the highest textile art. רֹקֵם (roqem) = "embroiderer" — three distinct craft titles for Oholiab. **LXX:** ἀρχιτέκτων... ὑφάντης... ποικιλτής — "master builder... weaver... variegator."

**24** All the gold [kol-ha-zahav] that was used [he-asui] for the work [la-melakhah] in all the work of [be-khol melekhet] the holy-place [ha-qodesh] — and the gold of [va-yehi zahav] the wave-offering [ha-tenufah] was [va-yehi] twenty-nine [tesha ve-esrim] talents [kikkar] and seven hundred and thirty [u-sheva me'ot u-sheloshim] shekels [sheqel], by the shekel of [be-sheqel] the holy-place [ha-qodesh].

> **Key terms:** כִּכָּר (kikkar) = "talent" — a round weight, approximately 34 kg (75 lbs). תְּנוּפָה (tenufah) = "wave-offering" — from נוף ("to wave"); the gold presented by elevation before YHWH. שֶׁקֶל הַקֹּדֶשׁ (sheqel ha-qodesh) = "shekel of the holy-place" — the sanctuary standard of weight. **LXX:** τάλαντα ("talents") — 29 talents and 730 shekels; Vaticanus matches the MT figures.

**25** And the silver of [ve-kesef] the numbered-ones of [pequdei] the congregation [ha-edah] — a hundred [me'at] talents [kikkar] and a thousand and seven hundred and seventy-five [ve-elef u-sheva me'ot va-khamishshah ve-shiv'im] shekels [sheqel], by the shekel of [be-sheqel] the holy-place [ha-qodesh].

> **Key terms:** פְקוּדֵי הָעֵדָה (pequdei ha-edah) = "the numbered/mustered-ones of the congregation" — silver collected from the census. 100 talents + 1,775 shekels. **LXX:** ἑκατὸν τάλαντα — matches.

**26** A bekah [biqa] per head [la-gulgoleth] — a half [makhatsuit] of the shekel [ha-sheqel] by the shekel of [be-sheqel] the holy-place [ha-qodesh] — for everyone [le-khol] who passed over [ha-over] to [al] the numbered-ones [ha-pequdim], from a son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] years [shanah] and upward [va-ma'lah], for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty [le-shesh me'ot elef u-sheloshet alafim va-khamesh me'ot va-khamishim].

> **Key terms:** בֶּקַע (biqa) = "bekah/half" — from בקע ("to split/cleave"); exactly half a shekel. לַגֻּלְגֹּלֶת (la-gulgoleth) = "per skull/head" — the per-capita levy. The total: 603,550 men — matching the census figure of Numbers 1:46. **LXX:** δραχμή ("drachma") for bekah — τοῖς παραπορευομένοις τὴν ἐπίσκεψιν ("those passing through the muster").

**27** And there were [va-yehi] the hundred [me'at] talents [kikkar] of the silver [ha-kesef] to cast [la-tseket] the bases of [et adnei] the holy-place [ha-qodesh] and the bases of [ve-et adnei] the curtain [ha-parokhet] — a hundred [me'ah] bases [adanim] for the hundred [le-me'at] talents [ha-kikkar] — a talent [kikkar] per base [la-adden].

> **Key terms:** לָצֶקֶת (la-tseket) = "to cast" — infinitive construct of יצק; the silver bases are cast, not hammered. פָּרֹכֶת (parokhet) = "curtain/veil" — the inner curtain separating the holy place from the most holy. One talent (~34 kg) per base — massive silver foundations. **LXX:** matches.

**28** And the thousand and seven hundred and seventy-five [ve-et-ha-elef u-sheva ha-me'ot va-khamishshah ve-shiv'im] he made [asah] hooks [vavim] for the pillars [la-ammudim], and he overlaid [ve-tsippah] their tops [rasheihem], and he banded them [ve-khishshaq otam].

> **Key terms:** The remaining 1,775 shekels (beyond the 100 talents) furnished the pillar hooks, capitals, and bands — every fraction of silver accounted for. **LXX:** matches.

**29** And the bronze of [u-nekoshet] the wave-offering [ha-tenufah] — seventy [shiv'im] talents [kikkar] and two thousand and four hundred [u-shnei alafim ve-arba me'ot] shekels [sheqel].

> **Key terms:** Bronze total: 70 talents + 2,400 shekels — dedicated by wave-offering as with the gold. **LXX:** matches.

**30** And he made [va-ya'as] with it [bah] the bases of [et adnei] the entrance of [petakh] the tent of meeting [ohel mo'ed], and the bronze altar [ve-et mizbach ha-nekhoshet], and the bronze grating [ve-et mikhbar ha-nekhoshet] that was for it [asher-lo], and all the vessels of [ve-et kol-klei] the altar [ha-mizbeakh],

> **Key terms:** The bronze allocation: tent-entrance bases, the altar itself, and its grating and implements — all from the 70-talent bronze supply. **LXX:** matches.

**31** and the bases of [ve-et adnei] the courtyard [he-khatser] all around [saviv], and the bases of [ve-et adnei] the gate of [sha'ar] the courtyard [he-khatser], and all the pegs of [ve-et kol-yitdot] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], and all the pegs of [ve-et kol-yitdot] the courtyard [he-khatser] all around [saviv].

> **Key terms:** יִתְדֹת (yitdot) = "pegs" — the final bronze items: courtyard bases and all anchoring pegs. The accounting is complete — every talent and shekel of gold, silver, and bronze has been assigned to specific components. No material is unaccounted for. **LXX:** matches, concluding the bronze inventory.

---

## Structural Notes

**Verse count:** 31 verses (MT). **Parashah:** This chapter spans the end of *Vayakhel* (vv. 1–20) and the opening of *Pekudei* (vv. 21–31), the final two parashot of Exodus.

**Two-part structure:** (1) Construction report (vv. 1–20): bronze altar, laver, courtyard — completing the outer-court furnishings; (2) Material accounting (vv. 21–31): a precise inventory linking every donation to its use.

**The census silver:** The 100 talents + 1,775 shekels from 603,550 men (at one bekah/half-shekel per head) is mathematically exact: 100 talents x 3,000 shekels = 300,000 shekels; 300,000 + 1,775 = 301,775 half-shekels; 301,775 x 2 = 603,550 men. The numbers are internally consistent.

**The women at the tent (v. 8):** The phrase מַרְאֹת הַצֹּבְאֹת (mar'ot ha-tsove'ot) is unique in the Torah. The women donated their bronze mirrors — instruments of self-regard — to create the laver of purification. The LXX's reading "fasting women" (νηστευσασῶν) reflects a different interpretive tradition, possibly connecting these women to ascetic practice at the sanctuary.

**MT-LXX divergence:** The LXX broadly aligns with the MT in this chapter (unlike the dramatic reordering seen in chs. 36–37). The bronze altar, courtyard, and accounting sections appear in recognizable form in Vaticanus. The most notable divergence remains the "fasting women" vs. "serving women" in v. 8.
